name: Robin AI
tagline: AI-powered contract review and negotiation platform for enterprise legal teams
description: Robin AI raised $71.7M to automate contract review for Fortune 500 companies but collapsed in 2025 when a critical $50M funding round fell through. Its managed services team was acquired by Scissero and its engineers were acqui-hired by Microsoft.
story:
Robin AI seemed to have it all. Founded in 2019 by former Clifford Chance lawyer Richard Robinson and machine learning researcher James Clough, the London-based startup built an AI-powered contract review tool that could scan incoming agreements, flag deviating clauses, and produce redlined documents in seconds. Blue-chip clients came knocking — PwC, KPMG, UBS, PepsiCo — and the funding followed. A $10.5M Series A led by SoftBank in 2023. A $26M Series B from Temasek in January 2024. A $25M extension round by November that same year, backed by PayPal Ventures and Cambridge University. Total raised: $71.7 million. In early 2025, Robin AI ranked 10th on the Sunday Times 100 Tech list.

Nine months later, it was listed for distressed sale.

The collapse was swift. A planned $50M raise failed to close. Losses had outpaced revenue growth for three years, despite $10M in annual recurring revenue. Leadership turnover — including the departure of co-founder and CTO James Clough — signaled deeper trouble. Staff were cut from 200 to roughly 100. Then HMRC filed a winding-up petition for unpaid taxes, and the runway disappeared entirely.

The company was carved up. Scissero, a rival legal services firm, acquired the managed services team of roughly 75 people in December 2025. Microsoft swooped in weeks later, acqui-hiring at least 18 engineers to bolster Word's legal AI capabilities. Robin AI's name joined the growing list of well-funded AI startups that scaled faster than their unit economics could sustain.

sources.title: Robin AI listed for distressed sale nine months after making the Sunday Times 100 Tech list
Scissero Acquires Robin AI's Managed Legal Services Amid Reported Insolvency Pressures
Is the Collapse of Robin AI a One-Off or a Sign of a Legal Tech AI Bubble?
